**Why.** A live projection of the same data the static report shows. Keep it
minimal — polish is cut-first.
**Scope.** LiveView rendering the hierarchy + ledger timeline; routes; basic
layout.
**Acceptance.** Renders a seeded Slice + its ledger timeline.
**Refs.** §21, §25 P0.9.

title: P0.9.2 Live updates via EventOutbox/PubSub
description:
**What.** Live updates from EventOutbox/PubSub.
**Why.** The viewer must update live as ledger events append — consuming the
outbox so it never shows an uncommitted transition.
**Scope.** subscribe to PubSub topics; patch assigns on new events.
**Acceptance.** A seeded Slice updates live when ledger events append.
**Refs.** §8.1, §21, §25 P0.9.

title: P0.1 — Project scaffold
description:
# P0.1 — Project scaffold

**What.** A booting Phoenix + Ash + AshPostgres + Oban application with the
supervision tree from §8, green CI, a pinned tooling matrix, and an
OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry skeleton.

**Why / background.** The BEAM is chosen for durable supervision, concurrent
orchestration, stronger compile-time checks, and later self-healing. Getting the
supervision topology and telemetry conventions right on day one avoids
backfilling trace_ids and metric dimensions once Phase 3 scheduling and Phase 6
cost observability arrive.

**Scope.** Conveyor.Application supervision tree (Repo, Oban, Endpoint,
Conductor.Supervisor children); CI (mix test, format --check, Credo/Dialyzer);
tested matrix Elixir 1.20.x / OTP 27+ recorded in evidence; telemetry span
hierarchy + metric cardinality rules.

**Acceptance.** App boots; CI is green; runtime versions are captured per run.

**Refs.** §2, §8, §8.1, §9.1, §25 P0.1. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (never-cut foundation).
close_reason: All P0.1 scaffold child beads are implemented. Verified Docker Elixir 1.20 CI-equivalent checks (format, compile, 11 tests, Credo, Dialyzer), runtime_versions capture, and Phoenix boot against Postgres 16.

title: P0.1.1 App boots + §8 supervision tree skeleton
description:
**What.** Boot a Phoenix+Ash+AshPostgres+Oban umbrella/app and stand up the §8
supervision tree skeleton.
**Why.** Durable supervision and a correct topology on day one. The
Conductor.Supervisor children (Ledger, Telemetry, Config, Policy.Engine,
Security.Redactor, Artifacts.Projector, EventOutbox, Effects.Reconciler,
Sandbox.Reaper) and the Oban worker modules should exist as supervised
skeletons even before full behavior lands.
**Scope.** mix project; Repo; Oban; Endpoint; Conductor.Supervisor with named
child skeletons; Oban worker module stubs (RunSlice, BaselineHealth, etc.).
**Acceptance.** `mix phx.server`/app boots cleanly; supervision tree matches §8.
**Refs.** §2, §8.
close_reason: Implemented Phoenix/Ash/AshPostgres/Oban scaffold with Conveyor.Application, Repo, Endpoint, Oban, Conveyor.Conductor.Supervisor children, and station Oban worker stubs. Verified under Docker elixir:1.20 (Elixir 1.20.1/OTP 29): mix format --check-formatted, mix compile, mix test (4 passed), and mix phx.server boot against Postgres 16 with Oban migrations.

title: P0.1.2 CI pipeline (test/format/credo/dialyzer)
description:
**What.** CI pipeline running mix test, mix format --check-formatted, Credo, and
Dialyzer (where configured).
**Why.** A green baseline from commit one; TDD the deterministic core means CI
must be trustworthy before any station logic lands.
**Scope.** CI workflow; format/credo/dialyzer config; cache for deps/PLTs.
**Acceptance.** CI is green on the empty scaffold.
**Refs.** §25 P0.1, §23.
close_reason: Implemented CI workflow for Elixir 1.20.1/OTP 29 with Postgres 16, dependency/_build/PLT cache, format, compile, tests, Credo, and Dialyzer. Added Credo/Dialyxir deps and configs. Verified locally in Docker elixir:1.20: mix format --check-formatted, mix compile --warnings-as-errors, MIX_ENV=test mix test (4 passed), mix credo --strict (no issues), mix dialyzer (passed with one narrow Phoenix upstream ignore).

title: P0.1.3 Tool matrix pinning + runtime version capture
description:
**What.** Pin the tested tool matrix and capture per-run runtime versions.
**Why.** Reproducibility first. Every run records the runtime that produced its
evidence so old evidence stays interpretable.
**Scope.** pin Elixir 1.20.x / OTP 27+, Phoenix/Ash/Oban versions; capture
elixir/otp/phoenix/ash/oban/docker_engine/sandbox_runner/agent_adapter versions +
toolchain_image_digest into RunSpec/evidence.
**Acceptance.** A run records all version fields from §9.1.
**Refs.** §2, §9.1.
close_reason: Pinned the tested Elixir 1.20/Phoenix/Ash/Oban dependency matrix, added runtime version capture, and required runtime_versions in RunSpec and Evidence schemas/examples. Verified schema registry examples, Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (7 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.1.4 Telemetry/OTel span hierarchy + metric cardinality rules
description:
**What.** OpenTelemetry-compatible telemetry skeleton + metric conventions.
**Why.** Traces are the most mature Elixir SDK signal; getting the span hierarchy
and metric cardinality rules right now avoids backfilling for Phase 3 scheduling
and Phase 6 cost observability.
**Scope.** span hierarchy (conveyor.run_slice → station.* → adapter.pi.session →
tool.command); trace_id propagation into LedgerEvent/StationRun/ToolInvocation/
artifacts; allowed metric dimensions only (project_id, station, adapter, profile,
status, failure_category, policy_profile, suite_kind); reject high-cardinality
labels.
**Acceptance.** Spans nest per §8.1; metrics reject disallowed dimensions.
**Refs.** §8.1.
close_reason: Implemented OpenTelemetry-compatible span hierarchy, trace context propagation helpers, allowed metric dimension validation, and guarded metric emission. Verified with Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (11 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2 — Config + doctor + init
description:
# P0.2 — Config + doctor + init

**What.** `.conveyor/config.toml` schema and loader; `mix conveyor.init` to
scaffold `.conveyor/` (config, policies, starter AGENTS.md, artifact dirs); and
`mix conveyor.doctor` to verify all prerequisites with actionable findings.

**Why / background.** An OSS-friendly factory needs crisp operator commands.
Doctor must fail HARD and ACTIONABLY when required sandbox constraints, policy
profiles, or test commands are missing, and only WARN for missing optional
future adapters. Every blocking finding must carry at least one suggested
NextAction and the exact Mix command to rerun.

**Scope.** config schema + Conveyor.Config; init scaffolding; doctor checks
(versions, Postgres, Oban, Docker rootless, Pi image, provider credential scope,
CodeScent if gate-blocking, git, sample repo cleanliness, AGENTS.md lint, test
commands, policy profiles, artifact dir writable, no prod secrets); stable exit
codes 0–7.

**Acceptance.** Missing Docker/Pi/CodeScent/Postgres/test-commands/policies are
reported clearly with NextActions; exit codes match §9.

**Refs.** §9, §9.1, §25 P0.2. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P0.2 config/init/doctor work: child beads q19.3.1-q19.3.4 closed; focused Elixir 1.20 + Postgres verification passed (config/init/doctor task tests: 12 passed).

title: P0.2.1 .conveyor/config.toml schema + Config loader
description:
**What.** `.conveyor/config.toml` schema + `Conveyor.Config` runtime/project
loader.
**Why.** Single source for project command specs, policy profile selection,
quality adapter choice, and artifact dirs. Language-specifics live in config, not
in core (portability rule §2).
**Scope.** config.toml schema (project command_specs, policies dir, prompts dir,
quality adapter, autonomy default); Config loader + validation; sample config.
**Acceptance.** Loader parses + validates; missing required keys produce clear
errors.
**Refs.** §2, §6.4 (.conveyor layout), §9.
close_reason: Implemented .conveyor/config.toml schema loader using latest toml_elixir 3.1.0, typed project/command config structs, validation errors with precise paths, and a reusable sample config template. Verified Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (15 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2.2 mix conveyor.init scaffolding
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.init SAMPLE_PROJECT_PATH`.
**Why.** Station 1 of the tracer (§22.4): scaffold the `.conveyor/` tree so a repo
is ready for the factory.
**Scope.** create .conveyor/ (config.toml, policies/{implement,verify}.toml,
prompts/{implementation-prompt@1,reviewer@1}.md, runs/, blobs/), starter
AGENTS.md (via P0.7 generator).
**Acceptance.** init creates the full .conveyor tree + starter AGENTS.md +
artifact dirs.
**Refs.** §6.4, §22.4 (1).
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.init to scaffold .conveyor/config.toml, policies, prompts, runs/blobs artifact directories, and starter AGENTS.md without overwriting existing files. Verified with Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (17 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P0.2.3 mix conveyor.doctor checks (§9.1)
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.doctor` with the full §9.1 check list.
**Why.** Doctor is the operator's first line of defense; it must fail hard when
required prerequisites are missing and only warn for optional future adapters.
**Scope.** checks: versions match matrix; Postgres; Oban; Docker reachable
(rootless preferred); Pi image; provider credential scope (live only); CodeScent
(only if gate-blocking); git; sample repo clean at base; AGENTS.md present +
lint-clean; test commands; policy profiles; artifact dir writable; no prod-looking
secrets mounted; report host sandbox capabilities.
**Acceptance.** Missing Docker/Postgres/test-commands/policies = failure; missing
optional adapter = warning.
**Refs.** §9.1, §12.3 (sandbox caps), §25 P0.2.
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.doctor and Conveyor.Doctor checks for versions, Oban, Postgres, Docker, git, AGENTS.md, verify command specs, policy profiles, artifact dirs, optional Pi/provider inputs, and secret posture. Verified missing Docker/Postgres/test commands/policies fail and optional adapter absence warns; Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (22 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer passed.

title: P0.2.4 NextAction findings + stable exit codes (0–7)
description:
**What.** Actionable NextAction findings + stable CLI exit codes across all
commands.
**Why.** Every blocking doctor/audit/gate finding must include at least one
suggested NextAction and the exact Mix command to rerun. Stable exit codes make
the CLI scriptable in CI.
**Scope.** NextAction struct (§6.3 findings.next_actions); exit codes 0–7 (§9);
shared finding rendering.
**Acceptance.** A failing doctor prints a NextAction + rerun command and exits
with the documented code.
**Refs.** §6.3, §9 (exit codes).
close_reason: Implemented shared NextAction and stable CLI exit code mapping, attached rerun actions to blocking doctor findings, and updated mix conveyor.doctor to halt with documented codes. Verified failing doctor output includes NextAction/rerun command and exits 7 for malformed config; Docker Elixir 1.20 format/compile/test (23 tests), Credo strict, and Dialyzer passed.

title: P0.3 — Ash domain & migrations
description:
# P0.3 — Ash domain & migrations

**What.** Define the active Ash/AshPostgres resources from §6.1 (and §8/§12.4
additions: StationEffect, CredentialLease), their migrations, embedded value
schemas (§6.3), database invariants/unique constraints (§6.5), and documented
schema specs for deferred resources (§6.2) WITHOUT creating their tables.

**Why / background.** One coherent Postgres source of truth where policies and
state transitions are enforceable. The schema must establish stable seams for
future decomposition, parallelism, policy, and learning without forcing those
features into the tracer bullet. Immutable fields (digests, base commits, locked
contracts, blob refs) must never update in place — corrections create new
records + ledger events.

**Scope (subtasks).** Project/Toolchain/Cache; Plan/Requirement/HumanDecision/
PlanAudit; Epic/Slice/DiffPolicy/ReviewPolicy; AgentBrief/ContractLock/TestPack/
VerificationSuite/TestPackCalibration; RunSpec + StationPlan; RunAttempt/
AgentSession/StationRun/StationEffect; PatchSet/RiskAssessment/Workspace; Evidence/
ToolInvocation/Review/GateResult; Artifact/RunBundle/ReviewerHealth/GateHealth;
ContextPack/InstructionSource/CodeQualityRun/RunPrompt; Policy/Retention/RunBudget/
Incident/CredentialLease/HumanApproval/ExternalChange/PatchEquivalence/LedgerEvent;
embedded schemas; DB invariants; deferred-resource specs.

**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; active resources CRUD through Ash; deferred
specs document future invariants without creating unused tables; unique
constraints from §6.5 are enforced at the DB layer.

**Refs.** §6, §6.1, §6.1.1, §6.2, §6.3, §6.5, §8 (StationEffect), §12.4
(CredentialLease), §25 P0.3. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Closed for the non-deferred scope: q19.4.1 through q19.4.13 are implemented and verified; the only remaining child q19.4.14 is labeled defer and is excluded per user instruction that existing deferred beads are out of scope for now. Latest full Elixir 1.20 gate during this pass passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 86 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.1 Project + ToolchainProfile + CacheMount
description:
**What.** Define Project, ToolchainProfile, CacheMount Ash resources + migrations.
**Why.** The project root identity, pinned toolchain (image digest, dependency
lock digest, cache policy, SBOM ref), and content-addressed cache mounts.
Reproducibility via digests, performance via SAFE read-only caches (not mutable
ambient state).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; AshPostgres migrations; basic CRUD actions.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; resources CRUD through Ash.
**Refs.** §6.1, §12.4 (image/cache).
close_reason: Implemented Project, ToolchainProfile, and CacheMount Ash resources with migrations and CRUD tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused resource test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 27 tests.

title: P0.3.10 ContextPack + InstructionSource + CodeQualityRun + RunPrompt
description:
**What.** Define ContextPack, InstructionSource, CodeQualityRun, RunPrompt.
**Why.** ContextPack = scout output (cited relevant files/risks/tests).
InstructionSource = trust-labeled prompt inputs (system/project/plan/brief/
agents_md/repo_file/tool_output → trusted/bounded/untrusted). CodeQualityRun =
quality adapter result + new_high_risk_findings. RunPrompt = the versioned,
immutable assembled prompt.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; InstructionSource trust_level enum enforced.
**Refs.** §6.1, §5 (instruction hierarchy), §13, §14.
close_reason: Implemented ContextPack, InstructionSource, CodeQualityRun, and RunPrompt Ash resources with migrations, relationships, InstructionSource trust/source enum constraints, and CRUD/enum tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused prompt context test passed with 3 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 61 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.11 Policy + Retention + RunBudget + Incident + CredentialLease + HumanApproval + ExternalChange + PatchEquivalence + LedgerEvent
description:
**What.** Define Policy, RetentionPolicy, RunBudget, Incident, CredentialLease,
HumanApproval, ExternalChange, PatchEquivalence, LedgerEvent.
**Why.** The safety/economic/integration/audit spine. Policy (profiles +
allow/deny/env/network/budget/autonomy ceiling). RetentionPolicy (artifact
sensitivity retention). RunBudget (caps + consumed counters). Incident
(violations). CredentialLease (short-lived scoped provider secrets). HumanApproval
+ ExternalChange + PatchEquivalence (manual integration recording). LedgerEvent
(append-only audit + idempotency_key unique).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1/§8/§12.4; unique(LedgerEvent.idempotency_key).
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; LedgerEvent idempotency unique; CredentialLease
status lifecycle present.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.5, §8, §12.4, §21.
close_reason: Implemented Policy, RetentionPolicy, RunBudget, Incident, CredentialLease, HumanApproval, ExternalChange, PatchEquivalence, and LedgerEvent resources with migrations, lifecycle/decision enum constraints, LedgerEvent idempotency uniqueness, and focused CRUD/uniqueness tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused safety audit test passed with 4 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 65 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.12 DB invariants + unique constraints + immutable-field guards
description:
**What.** Enforce the §6.5 database invariants + immutable-field guards.
**Why.** Idempotency and evidence integrity deserve DATABASE backing, not just
Ash validations. Immutable fields (digests, base commits, locked contracts, blob
refs) must never update in place — corrections create new records + ledger events.
**Scope.** all unique constraints from §6.5 as DB constraints; check
constraints/triggers or Ash change guards preventing in-place mutation of
immutable fields; StationEffect uniqueness.
**Acceptance.** Attempting to mutate an immutable digest fails; duplicate
idempotency keys rejected at DB layer.
**Refs.** §6.5.
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (C4): add two explicit acceptance criteria -- (1) at most one active (planned/running) RunAttempt per slice enforced at the DB layer (section 6.5); (2) in-place mutation of an immutable field (digests, base commits, locked contracts, blob refs) is rejected; corrections create new records + ledger events.
close_reason: Implemented DB-backed immutable-field guards with a PostgreSQL trigger function across digest, base commit, blob, bundle, gate freshness, patch equivalence, and ledger identity fields. Verified unique constraints from §6.5 remain DB-backed, one-active RunAttempt is enforced, StationEffect/LedgerEvent idempotency is enforced, and immutable updates fail while mutable fields can still change. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused DB invariants test passed with 3 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 68 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.13 Embedded value schemas (AC / command_specs / findings / risk_rules)
description:
**What.** Implement the §6.3 typed embedded value schemas.
**Why.** Future concepts appear as typed embedded JSON inside active resources
before becoming first-class tables. These shared shapes are validated everywhere.
**Scope.** acceptance_criteria[] (id/text/kind/requirement_refs/required_test_refs/
evidence_status/evidence_refs); command_specs[] / ToolInvocation.command_spec;
findings[] (severity/category/message/next_actions); ReviewPolicy.risk_rules[].
**Acceptance.** Each embedded schema validates; invalid enum values rejected.
**Refs.** §6.3.
close_reason: Implemented shared embedded-schema validations for acceptance_criteria, command_specs/ToolInvocation.command_spec, findings, and ReviewPolicy risk_rules while preserving the existing JSON/map storage shape. Added focused enum rejection tests for each schema family and updated existing resource tests to use valid embedded values. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused embedded schema test passed with 5 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 73 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.3.14 Deferred-resource schema specs (docs/future-schemas/)
description:
**What.** Document deferred-resource schema specs under docs/future-schemas/
WITHOUT creating tables.
**Why.** Deferred resources should have schema sketches, invariants, and expected
event types so future phases have a clean seam — but no migrations until a phase
uses them (promotion rule §6.2).
**Scope.** WorkspacePool, TaskClaim, MergeQueueItem, BudgetLedger,
AgentReputation, Memory, ExternalTaskRef — schema sketch + invariants + event
types each.
**Acceptance.** Specs exist; no unused tables created.
**Refs.** §6.2.

title: P0.3.2 Plan + Requirement + HumanDecision + PlanAudit
description:
**What.** Define Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, PlanAudit resources.
**Why.** Plan quality is a first-class gate. Requirements carry stable keys +
source spans + contract digests for traceability; HumanDecision records explicit
choices; PlanAudit records score/decision/findings/coverage.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; unique(plan_id, stable_key) for Requirement &
HumanDecision (§6.5); normalized_contract + contract_sha256 on Plan.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; unique keys enforced; CRUD works.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.5, §10.
close_reason: Implemented Plan, Requirement, HumanDecision, and PlanAudit Ash resources with migrations, CRUD tests, and DB unique indexes for requirement/decision stable keys. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 31 tests.

title: P0.3.3 Epic + Slice + DiffPolicy + ReviewPolicy
description:
**What.** Define Epic, Slice, DiffPolicy, ReviewPolicy resources.
**Why.** The work graph (Epic→Slice) plus the policies that bound a Slice's diff
scope (allowed/protected globs, size caps, dependency/migration/public-API
allowances) and review requirements (risk_rules → required review kinds +
escalation).
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; Slice carries autonomy_level, source_refs,
likely_files, conflict_domains (swarm-readiness §27); unique(epic_id, position).
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; Slice records swarm-readiness fields; unique
position enforced.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.3 (risk_rules), §6.5, §27.
close_reason: Implemented Epic, Slice, DiffPolicy, and ReviewPolicy Ash resources with migrations, CRUD tests, Slice swarm-readiness fields, and unique(epic_id, position). Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 35 tests.

title: P0.3.4 AgentBrief + ContractLock + TestPack + VerificationSuite + TestPackCalibration
description:
**What.** Define AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, VerificationSuite,
TestPackCalibration.
**Why.** The contract spine. AgentBrief is THE contract (ACs, required tests,
verification commands, locked). ContractLock freezes the digests the gate checks
against. TestPack is the locked, read-only acceptance test bundle.
VerificationSuite classifies suites (baseline_regression/acceptance_locked/...) +
expected_on_base/patch. TestPackCalibration records red/green calibration.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; unique(slice_id, version) for AgentBrief & TestPack;
all the *_sha256 lock fields on ContractLock.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; lock digests stored immutably.
**Refs.** §6.0 (contract evolution), §6.1, §6.5.
close_reason: Implemented AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, VerificationSuite, and TestPackCalibration Ash resources with migrations, unique(slice_id, version) constraints for AgentBrief/TestPack, and immutable no-update locked digest records for ContractLock/TestPack. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 5 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 40 tests.

title: P0.3.5 RunSpec capsule + StationPlan embedded schema
description:
**What.** Define the RunSpec immutable execution capsule + embedded StationPlan.
**Why.** RunSpec is the content-addressed answer to "what exactly did this run try
to do?" — it freezes project/base commit, all contract/policy/test-pack/agents-md
digests, agent profile snapshot, image digest, sandbox profile, budget, canary
suite version, schema versions, and the station-plan digest. StationPlan is a
versioned immutable station DAG (linear in Phase 1, parallel-ready schema).
**Scope.** fields per §6.0/§6.1; unique(run_spec_sha256); StationPlan embedded
schema (conveyor.station_plan@1); every station input/output includes
run_spec_sha256.
**Acceptance.** RunSpec is immutable; a mutable upstream change forces a NEW
RunSpec (no silent reuse); station_plan validates.
**Refs.** §6.0, §6.1, §6.5.
close_reason: Implemented immutable RunSpec Ash resource with unique run_spec_sha256, embedded station_plan map, and conveyor.station_plan@1 validation requiring run_spec_sha256 on every station input/output. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 3 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 43 tests.

title: P0.3.6 RunAttempt + AgentSession + StationRun + StationEffect
description:
**What.** Define RunAttempt, AgentSession, StationRun, StationEffect resources.
**Why.** RunAttempt is the parent identity for one Slice execution attempt (all
stations belong to the attempt, not the agent session). AgentSession is adapter
output (untrusted transcript). StationRun is per-station progress w/ leases +
idempotency keys. StationEffect declares external side effects for crash-safe
reconciliation.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1/§8; unique(slice_id, attempt_no);
unique(StationRun.idempotency_key); unique(StationEffect.idempotency_key); at most
one planned/running RunAttempt per slice in Phase 1.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; idempotency keys unique; one-active-attempt
invariant enforced.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.5, §8.
close_reason: Implemented RunAttempt, AgentSession, StationRun, and StationEffect Ash resources with migrations, unique(slice_id, attempt_no), partial one-active-attempt-per-slice index, and station/effect idempotency-key uniqueness. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 47 tests.

title: P0.3.7 PatchSet + RiskAssessment + WorkspaceMaterialization
description:
**What.** Define PatchSet, RiskAssessment, WorkspaceMaterialization.
**Why.** PatchSet is the captured diff (from fresh git diff, never uncommitted
state) with scope metrics + touches_locked_paths + applies_cleanly.
RiskAssessment compares planned vs observed risk → required reviews/gate stages.
WorkspaceMaterialization tracks each checkout (baseline/calibration/implement/
gate/canary/post_integration) + cleanup lifecycle.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; head_tree_sha256; cleanup_policy/cleanup_status.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; PatchSet records scope + locked-path touch flag.
**Refs.** §6.1.
close_reason: Implemented PatchSet, RiskAssessment, and WorkspaceMaterialization Ash resources with migrations and CRUD tests; PatchSet records scope metrics, applies_cleanly, and touches_locked_paths. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 3 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 50 tests.

title: P0.3.8 Evidence + ToolInvocation + Review + GateResult
description:
**What.** Define Evidence, ToolInvocation, Review, GateResult.
**Why.** The core evidence/verdict resources. Evidence ties patch→AC results→
quality→pr_body. ToolInvocation records every command (argv, cwd, env keys,
network, output refs, policy decision). Review is the reviewer verdict (schema
§19). GateResult records stages + pass + false_negative + all freshness digests.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; GateResult carries gate_version/gate_code_sha256/
policy_sha256/contract_lock_sha256/canary_suite_version.
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; CRUD works; GateResult stores freshness keys.
**Refs.** §6.1, §17, §19.
close_reason: Implemented Evidence, ToolInvocation, Review, and GateResult Ash resources with migrations and CRUD tests. GateResult stores gate_version, gate_code_sha256, policy_sha256, contract_lock_sha256, and canary_suite_version freshness keys. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused test 4 passed; full suite format/compile/test/Credo/Dialyzer passed with 54 tests.

title: P0.3.9 Artifact + RunBundle + ReviewerHealth + GateHealth
description:
**What.** Define Artifact, RunBundle, ReviewerHealth, GateHealth.
**Why.** Artifact = content-addressed blob identity (sha256) + projection path +
sensitivity + schema_version. RunBundle = canonical run-directory manifest with
bundle_root_sha256 (Conveyor's first trust anchor). ReviewerHealth/GateHealth =
the single queryable freshness/honesty summaries the gate consults.
**Scope.** fields per §6.1; unique(Artifact.sha256, size_bytes);
unique(GateHealth.project_id, freshness_key_sha256).
**Acceptance.** Migrations apply; uniqueness enforced; sensitivity enum present.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.4, §17 (freshness), §19.
close_reason: Implemented Artifact, RunBundle, ReviewerHealth, and GateHealth Ash resources with migrations and CRUD/uniqueness tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused artifact health test passed with 4 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 58 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.4 — Plan audit + traceability
description:
# P0.4 — Plan audit + traceability

**What.** The deterministic plan compiler-as-audit: parse + JSON-Schema-validate
the normalized `conveyor.plan@1` contract (sidecar `conveyor.plan.yml` or fenced
block), import Requirements/HumanDecisions with stable keys, build forward/back
traceability, score readiness, and emit deterministic blocking findings via
`mix conveyor.plan_audit`. Includes the Plan state machine (§7.1).

**Why / background.** A Slice must not reach an agent merely because a human
typed a Brief. This tests the FUTURE plan compiler without building agentic
generation yet. Design law 9: no orphan requirements and no orphan Slices. The
audit need not be smart — it must be strict, deterministic, and loud about
ambiguity (false positives acceptable if findings are actionable).

**Scope.** contract parser + schema validation; Requirement/HumanDecision import;
traceability engine (req→AC→test; slice→req/decision/bug) + orphan detection;
scoring + findings + decision (ready/needs_clarification/blocked); CLI +
readiness report; Plan state machine; plan_audit eval fixtures.

**Acceptance.** Good sample plan with a valid contract is handoff_ready;
prose-only, vague, missing, or untraceable plans are blocked with stable findings.

**Refs.** §7.1, §9.2, §10, §25 P0.4, §18 (plan_audit suite). Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P0.4 plan audit and traceability stack across q19.5.1-q19.5.7: contract parser/schema validation, stable-key imports, traceability/orphan detection, PlanAudit scoring/persistence, mix conveyor.plan_audit CLI, Plan lifecycle transitions, and eval fixtures. Latest verification on q19.5.7 passed Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 114 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.1 Normalized plan contract parser + schema validation
description:
**What.** Parse + JSON-Schema-validate the normalized conveyor.plan@1 contract.
**Why.** The human-readable markdown is NOT the execution contract. Phase 1
requires a sidecar conveyor.plan.yml or a fenced conveyor-plan@1 block; the
narrative explains intent, the normalized contract is what Conveyor validates,
locks, hashes, traces.
**Scope.** read sidecar/fenced block; validate against docs/schemas/
conveyor.plan@1.json; compute contract_sha256; explicit failures
(unsupported_schema_version, schema_validation_failed) — never best-effort parse.
**Acceptance.** Valid contract parses + hashes; prose-only plan is linted but
cannot become handoff_ready; unknown schema fails explicitly.
**Refs.** §10.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.PlanContract for normalized conveyor.plan@1 loading from sidecar conveyor.plan.yml/.yaml/.json or fenced conveyor-plan@1 markdown blocks, strict schema_version handling, JSV draft 2020-12 JSON Schema validation, and canonical contract_sha256 hashing. Added tests for valid JSON, sidecar YAML, fenced markdown, stable hash, prose-only rejection, missing schema_version, and schema validation errors. Verified focused plan contract tests: 7 passed. Full Elixir 1.20 gate passed: format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 93 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.4.2 Requirement + HumanDecision import (stable keys)
description:
**What.** Import Requirements + HumanDecisions with stable keys from the contract.
**Why.** Every Requirement has a stable key (REQ-001) and source section; every
HumanDecision a stable key. These anchor traceability and contract digests.
**Scope.** upsert by (plan_id, stable_key); record section_ref/source_span/
contract_sha256; status enum covered/deferred/out_of_scope/open.
**Acceptance.** Re-import is idempotent on stable keys; open requirements are
flagged.
**Refs.** §6.1, §10.
close_reason: Implemented stable-key Requirement and HumanDecision import via Conveyor.PlanImport with idempotent Ash upserts, open requirement summary, human decision trace fields, migration, and tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 95 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.3 Traceability engine + orphan detection
description:
**What.** Traceability engine + orphan detection.
**Why.** Design law 9: no orphan requirements and no orphan Slices. Requirements
map forward to Slices; Slices map back to a requirement/decision/bug/improvement;
ACs map to requirements; required tests map to ACs.
**Scope.** build req→AC→test forward map; slice→source back map; detect
orphans/open requirements; coverage summary.
**Acceptance.** A requirement with no Slice/Brief coverage and a Slice with no
source both produce blocking findings.
**Refs.** §3 (law 9), §9.2, §10.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.Traceability with requirement-to-AC-to-test maps, slice source maps, open requirement and orphan Slice blocking findings, coverage summary, and focused tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 98 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.4 PlanAudit scoring + findings + decision
description:
**What.** PlanAudit scoring + deterministic findings + decision.
**Why.** Audit must be strict, deterministic, and loud about ambiguity — the
smallest seed of the later plan-to-task compiler. False positives acceptable if
findings are actionable.
**Scope.** scores (clarity/acceptance coverage/testability/traceability/
architecture/autonomy readiness); findings categories (§9.2 list); decision
ready/needs_clarification/blocked; persist PlanAudit.
**Acceptance.** Deterministic, stable findings for the same input; decision maps
to score + blocking findings.
**Refs.** §9.2, §10, §20 (Plan Audit Failure).
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.PlanAuditor with deterministic component scores, merged findings, ready/needs_clarification/blocked decision mapping, persisted PlanAudit records, and tests for ready, blocked, and autonomy findings. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 101 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.5 mix conveyor.plan_audit CLI + readiness report
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.plan_audit PLAN.md` CLI + readiness report.
**Why.** Operator surface for the audit; output is the §9.2 readiness report.
**Scope.** CLI command; render scores + decision + blocking findings + NextActions;
exit code 2 on blocked.
**Acceptance.** Prints the §9.2 report shape; exits 0 on handoff_ready, 2 on
blocked.
**Refs.** §9, §9.2.
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.plan_audit PLAN with contract loading, plan/project/import/audit persistence, readiness report rendering, and exit code 2 for non-ready audits. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 103 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.6 Plan state machine (§7.1)
description:
**What.** Plan state machine (§7.1).
**Why.** A Phase-1 plan can be manually authored but still must pass audit before
the Slice can run — prevents treating manual input as automatically executable.
**Scope.** draft→audited→handoff_ready→active→completed; needs_clarification;
archived; ledger event per transition.
**Acceptance.** Legal transitions succeed; a plan cannot reach handoff_ready
without a passing audit.
**Refs.** §7.1.
close_reason: Implemented Plan lifecycle statuses, DB status constraint migration, guarded transition validation, handoff_ready audit guard, PlanLifecycle transition service, ledger event append per transition, and lifecycle tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 107 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.4.7 plan_audit eval fixtures + snapshot tests
description:
**What.** plan_audit eval fixtures.
**Why.** The plan_audit eval suite: vague, missing, contradictory, or untraceable
plans must be blocked; a good plan must pass.
**Scope.** labeled fixtures (good / missing-AC / missing-test / untraceable /
prose-only / contradictory) + expected decisions; snapshot tests.
**Acceptance.** Good plan passes; each bad fixture fails with stable findings.
**Refs.** §18 (plan_audit suite), §23.
close_reason: Implemented plan_audit eval fixtures and snapshot tests for good, missing-AC, missing-test, untraceable, vague, prose-only, and contradictory plans; relaxed required_test_refs to allow audit-level missing-test findings; added deterministic contradiction and orphan-slice outputs. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full gate: format, compile --warnings-as-errors, 114 tests, Credo strict, Dialyzer.

title: P0.5 — Slice state machine + ledger
description:
# P0.5 — Slice state machine + ledger

**What.** The Slice and RunAttempt state machines (§7.2, §7.3) with transition
guards, the append-only `LedgerEvent` with idempotency + one-event-per-transition
invariant, the transactional EventOutbox (§8.1), and R0/R1 replay.

**Why / background.** Design law 5: no hidden state — every material transition
and gate result appends a LedgerEvent. The Slice tracks the product/work-item
lifecycle; station-level progress lives on RunAttempt/StationRun so a Slice never
ping-pongs through implementation internals for a second attempt or re-review.
The outbox guarantees no observer (LiveView/telemetry/webhook) sees a transition
that failed to commit.

**Scope.** Slice SM + guards (plan readiness, Brief lock, actor separation,
required artifacts, gate stage completeness, autonomy policy); RunAttempt SM;
LedgerEvent semantics; EventOutbox + PubSub; R0 replay (timeline) via
`mix conveyor.replay`; R1 replay (artifact regeneration); state-machine tests.

**Acceptance.** Legal transitions succeed, illegal fail, every material
transition appends exactly one ledger event, R0/R1 replay regenerates timeline
and artifact projection.

**Refs.** §7.2, §7.3, §6.4 (R0/R1/R2), §8.1, §25 P0.5. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (ledger never-cut).

title: P0.5.1 Slice state machine + guards
description:
**What.** Slice state machine (§7.2) with transition guards (ash_state_machine).
**Why.** Slice tracks the product/work-item lifecycle (not internal stations).
Guards enforce plan readiness, Brief lock, actor separation, required artifacts,
gate stage completeness, autonomy policy.
**Scope.** drafted/approved→ready→in_progress→gated→integrated→done + needs_rework/
parked/failed/policy_blocked off-ramps; guards; ledger event per transition.
**Acceptance.** Legal transitions succeed; illegal fail; guards reject premature
transitions.
**Refs.** §7.2.
close_reason: Implemented Slice AshStateMachine transitions, guarded SliceLifecycle ledger transitions, migrations, and tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 121 tests, credo --strict, dialyzer.

title: P0.5.2 RunAttempt state machine (§7.3)
description:
**What.** RunAttempt state machine (§7.3).
**Why.** RunAttempt owns station-level progression so a Slice doesn't ping-pong
through internals on retries. One active attempt per Slice in Phase 1.
**Scope.** planned→running→evidence_recorded→reviewed→gated→reported + failed/
cancelled/stale/needs_rework/rejected; ledger events.
**Acceptance.** A failed implementation creates RunAttempt #2 against a fresh
RunSpec without mutating the Slice through every internal state.
**Refs.** §7.3.
close_reason: Implemented RunAttempt AshStateMachine transitions, RunAttemptLifecycle ledger transitions and retry creation from a fresh RunSpec, active-attempt migration, and execution tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 123 tests, credo --strict, dialyzer.

title: P0.5.3 LedgerEvent append-only semantics + idempotency
description:
**What.** LedgerEvent append-only semantics + idempotency + one-event-per-
transition invariant.
**Why.** Design law 5: no hidden state. The ledger is an immutable audit log and
timeline (not the sole state store in Phase 1).
**Scope.** Conveyor.Ledger writer; idempotency_key; trace_id/span_id; transition+
event in the same DB transaction for DB-local effects; deletion tombstones carry
artifact id + prior digest + reason + actor.
**Acceptance.** Every material transition appends exactly one event; duplicate
idempotency keys are no-ops.
**Refs.** §6.4, §6.5, §7, §8.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.Ledger idempotent writer, append-only ledger DB trigger, tombstone helper, lifecycle routing through the writer, transactional Plan transitions, and ledger tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 126 tests, credo --strict, dialyzer.

title: P0.5.4 EventOutbox transactional publication + PubSub
description:
**What.** Transactional EventOutbox + PubSub publication.
**Why.** Publication rule (§8.1): LedgerEvent is committed BEFORE publication;
observers consume from a transactional outbox so no one sees a transition that
failed to commit.
**Scope.** EventOutbox table + relay; Phoenix.PubSub broadcast; consumers
(LiveView/telemetry/webhooks) read the outbox, not raw inserts.
**Acceptance.** A rolled-back transaction publishes nothing; a committed event is
published exactly once.
**Refs.** §8, §8.1.
close_reason: Implemented EventOutbox resource/table, transactional LedgerEvent outbox enqueue, PubSub relay with publish-once behavior, and rollback/no-publication tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 129 tests, credo --strict, dialyzer.

title: P0.5.5 R0 replay (timeline) + mix conveyor.replay
description:
**What.** R0 replay — rebuild the human timeline from LedgerEvent — via
`mix conveyor.replay`.
**Why.** Replay level R0 (Phase 1): reconstruct the timeline from the append-only
ledger.
**Scope.** replay command; deterministic ordering by occurred_at + sequence.
**Acceptance.** Timeline regenerates identically from the event log.
**Refs.** §6.4 (R0), §9.
close_reason: Implemented R0 replay timeline projection and mix conveyor.replay JSONL CLI with deterministic occurred_at/id ordering. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 132 tests, credo --strict, dialyzer.

title: P0.5.6 R1 replay (artifact projection regeneration)
description:
**What.** R1 replay — regenerate the .conveyor/runs/<id>/ artifact projection from
DB records + content-addressed blobs.
**Why.** Replay level R1 (Phase 1): artifacts are a regenerable projection;
Postgres + blobs are truth. (R2 event-sourced state reconstruction is deferred.)
**Scope.** wire replay to the Artifacts.Projector (P0.8); verify blob digests
before regenerating.
**Acceptance.** Regenerating from the same records recreates the same tree +
checksums.
**Refs.** §6.4 (R1).
close_reason: Implemented R1 artifact replay via Conveyor.Artifacts.Projector and mix conveyor.replay RUN_ATTEMPT_ID. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 136 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.5.7 State-machine + ledger tests
description:
**What.** State-machine + ledger tests.
**Why.** Invariants must be tested, not aspirational. Legal transitions succeed,
illegal fail, guards enforced, exactly one ledger event per transition.
**Scope.** ExUnit for Slice + RunAttempt SMs; guard rejection cases; one-event
assertion; outbox publish-after-commit test.
**Acceptance.** All legal/illegal/guard cases covered; CI green.
**Refs.** §23 (state-machine tests).
close_reason: Expanded state-machine and ledger invariant coverage for Slice and RunAttempt lifecycles, including off-ramps, guard rollback/no ledger writes, exactly one outbox row per ledger event, and retry guard rejection. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 138 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.5.8 Station execution behaviour + StationRun lease/idempotency/effect contract
description:
**What.** A reusable `Conveyor.Station` behaviour/macro implementing the §8 station-job execution contract that every station (scout, baseline/calibration, implement, evidence, review, gate, canary) builds on — so station mechanics are defined ONCE, not re-derived per station or retrofitted from the orchestrator.
**Why.** Building 8 station workers before the shared execution seam exists invites ad-hoc per-station mechanics and a costly retrofit. The StationRun/StationEffect RESOURCES exist early (P0.3.6), but the BEHAVIOUR that uses them must too. Extracting this seam early is the "lay clean seams" discipline (§0.1).
**Scope.** the §8 8-rule station contract (load immutable inputs by digest; acquire/refresh lease + heartbeat; declare StationEffect before executing; write content-addressed artifacts; persist output digests; append LedgerEvent + transition in one DB txn for DB-local effects; reconcile declared effects; safe-to-retry without assuming exactly-once external execution); idempotency-key formula (run_attempt_id + station_key + station_spec_sha256 + attempt_no); the behaviour/macro the station workers implement. Station LOGIC stays independently testable; this standardizes the EXECUTION wrapper.
**Acceptance.** A station implemented on the behaviour acquires/refreshes a lease, declares effects before executing, and is safe to retry by idempotency key (no duplicate artifacts/ledger events/transitions).
**Refs.** §8, §8.1. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
[Bead-review addition 2026-06-17 — finding A1: extracted from the former monolithic iqb.14.1 so all station beads build on a shared execution seam.]

title: P0.6 — Policy engine
description:
# P0.6 — Policy engine

**What.** The ExecPolicy layer: policy profiles, structured command grammar +
path normalization, denylist/allowlist + env/network policy, the `ToolExecutor`
(the only component allowed to execute pre-exec-policy commands), ToolInvocation
recording, Incident creation, and RunBudget caps incl. non-progress detection.

**Why / background.** Docker limits blast radius; policy limits INTENT — both are
required (design law 8). Pi (and any adapter) is NOT the security boundary.
Conveyor owns tool execution; the sandbox owns fs/process/network limits; the
CredentialBroker owns provider access. Policy false positives are acceptable in
Phase 1; silent policy bypasses are not.

**Scope.** profiles (explore/implement/verify/release/maintenance); command
grammar + normalization + evaluation order (§12.2.1); denylist (§12.2) +
allowlist + env/network; ToolExecutor; ToolInvocation recording (argv, cwd, env
keys, network, output refs, policy decision); Incident + run stop + Slice
policy_blocked/failed; RunBudget + non-progress/budget exhaustion; policy eval
fixtures.

**Acceptance.** Dangerous command fixtures are blocked before execution where the
adapter supports pre-exec enforcement; all command attempts are recorded; a
violation creates an Incident and stops the run.

**Refs.** §12, §12.1, §12.2, §12.2.1, §12.5, §25 P0.6, §18 (policy suite). Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED (never-cut).

title: P0.6.1 Policy profiles (explore/implement/verify/release/maintenance)
description:
**What.** Policy profiles (§12.1).
**Why.** Policy limits intent in layers; profiles bound what a station may do.
**Scope.** explore/implement/verify/release/maintenance profiles as Policy
records (allowlist/denylist/env_policy/network_policy/budget_policy/
autonomy_ceiling); load from .conveyor/policies/*.toml.
**Acceptance.** Profiles load; implement allows workspace edits; verify forbids
source edits except tool cache; release/maintenance are future-gated.
**Refs.** §12.1.
close_reason: Implemented policy profile loading from .conveyor/policies/*.toml into Policy records, added all five profile templates, aligned maintenance profile naming, and updated init/doctor checks. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 141 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.6.2 Command grammar + path normalization
description:
**What.** Command grammar + path normalization (NormalizedCommand).
**Why.** Prefer structured command execution over shell. Normalize executable/
argv/cwd/env_keys/network/write_roots/read_roots/timeout before any policy
decision.
**Scope.** NormalizedCommand struct (§12.2.1); resolve executable inside
container; normalize cwd/symlinks/write roots; reject raw shell unless profile
allows.
**Acceptance.** A command is normalized deterministically; symlink-escapes from
write roots are rejected.
**Refs.** §12.2.1.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.Policy.NormalizedCommand with deterministic command-spec normalization, raw shell rejection, workspace cwd normalization, read/write root normalization, and symlink escape rejection. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 145 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.6.3 Denylist/allowlist + env/network policy (eval order)
description:
**What.** Denylist/allowlist + env/network policy evaluation order.
**Why.** Defense-in-depth: allow only configured executable families per profile,
then apply the denylist. Phase-1 defaults allow only sample-project families
(python/pytest/git diff-status/rg/quality tools).
**Scope.** evaluation order (§12.2.1 steps 1–7); minimum denylist (§12.2: rm -rf,
git reset --hard, force push, curl|sh, sudo, ssh/cloud creds, prod DB URLs,
network except allowlist, any deploy at L0–L2); env allowlist; record decision
before execution.
**Acceptance.** Each denylist class is blocked; only allowed families run; every
decision recorded pre-exec.
**Refs.** §12.2, §12.2.1.

title: P0.6.4 ToolExecutor (sole pre-exec-policy command path)
description:
**What.** `ToolExecutor` — the only component allowed to execute commands for
stations that require pre-exec policy.
**Why.** Adapter-reported command execution is NOT trusted unless it passed
through ToolExecutor. This is the determinism/safety chokepoint.
**Scope.** ToolExecutor that takes a NormalizedCommand + PolicyProfile, asks
Policy.Engine for a decision, executes via SandboxRunner.exec, records a
ToolInvocation.
**Acceptance.** A command not routed through ToolExecutor cannot be counted as a
trusted invocation; policy decision precedes execution.
**Refs.** §12.5.

title: P0.6.5 ToolInvocation recording
description:
**What.** ToolInvocation recording.
**Why.** Every command attempt is recorded with full provenance for evidence +
swarm-readiness.
**Scope.** record tool_name, command_spec, profile, cwd, env_keys, network_mode,
exit_code, duration_ms, stdout_ref/stderr_ref, output_sha256, policy_decision,
status.
**Acceptance.** Every executed and every BLOCKED command produces a ToolInvocation.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.3, §12.2.

title: P0.6.6 Incident creation + run stop + Slice transition
description:
**What.** Incident creation + run stop + Slice transitions on violation.
**Why.** A policy violation creates an Incident, stops the run, records evidence,
and moves the Slice to policy_blocked or failed by severity. Silent bypasses are
unacceptable.
**Scope.** Incident record; stop-the-run hook; transition to policy_blocked/
failed; ledger event.
**Acceptance.** A dangerous-command fixture creates an Incident and blocks the run.
**Refs.** §12.2, §20 (Policy Failure).

title: P0.6.7 RunBudget caps + non-progress detection
description:
**What.** RunBudget caps + non-progress / budget-exhaustion detection.
**Why.** Budget exhaustion is a policy-controlled STOP, not an ordinary failure.
Non-progress is also budget exhaustion.
**Scope.** caps (wall clock, idle, tool calls, commands, output bytes, repeated
commands, same-file rewrites, no-diff-progress, tokens/cost if available);
detect repeated identical failures / no diff progress / output flooding /
heartbeat-without-progress; structured finding + Slice needs_rework/parked/failed.
**Acceptance.** Exceeding a cap stops the run with consumed-counter findings.
**Refs.** §12.2 (budget), §24 (runaway cost).

title: P0.6.8 policy eval fixtures + tests
description:
**What.** policy eval fixtures.
**Why.** The policy eval suite: dangerous commands, env access, network attempts,
and protected-path edits must be blocked or recorded per profile.
**Scope.** labeled fixtures per denylist class + expected outcome; tests asserting
Incident creation + block.
**Acceptance.** Each dangerous fixture is blocked/recorded as expected.
**Refs.** §18 (policy suite), §23 (policy tests).

title: P0.7 — AGENTS.md generator + linter
description:
# P0.7 — AGENTS.md generator + linter

**What.** Generate a repo-local, agent-readable `AGENTS.md` from
`Project.command_specs`, and lint it for required sections, command consistency,
measurable done criteria, and policy alignment.

**Why / background.** The factory should not hand a repo to any coding agent
without a clear repo-local contract. The linter keeps AGENTS.md from drifting out
of sync with project config and the Policy denylist.

**Scope.** generator (min structure §11); linter checks (required commands match
Project specs; done criteria mention evidence + independent verification;
forbidden actions align with Policy denylist; no contradictions with configured
commands; security rules forbid prod secrets/deploys; ambiguous terms absent);
linter tests.

**Acceptance.** Generated file passes; an intentionally incomplete/contradictory
file fails with useful findings.

**Refs.** §11, §25 P0.7. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed AGENTS.md generator, linter, and fixture coverage. Generator emits the required repo-local contract from .conveyor/config.toml, linter validates required sections/commands/done criteria/security/denylist/contradictions/ambiguity, and fixtures cover complete, missing-command, vague-done, contradictory, and no-security cases. Latest full Elixir 1.20 verification passed: format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 82 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.7.1 AGENTS.md generator
description:
**What.** AGENTS.md generator.
**Why.** The factory must not hand a repo to any agent without a repo-local
contract. Commands come from .conveyor/config.toml so the file never drifts from
project config.
**Scope.** generate the §11 min structure (Overview, Architecture Map, Commands,
Coding/Testing/Security/Git/Task Rules, Done Criteria, Forbidden Actions, How to
Use Conveyor Evidence / CodeScent Context / Report Blockers).
**Acceptance.** Generated file contains all required sections with commands drawn
from config.
**Refs.** §11.
close_reason: Implemented AGENTS.md generation from .conveyor/config.toml via Conveyor.AgentsMd and mix conveyor.agents, and updated conveyor.init to generate AGENTS.md without overwriting existing files. Generated output includes all required §11 sections and command lines rendered from config. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused AGENTS tests passed with 5 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 76 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.7.2 AGENTS.md linter
description:
**What.** AGENTS.md linter.
**Why.** Keep the contract honest and aligned with project config + Policy
denylist; reject ambiguity.
**Scope.** checks (§11): required command sections; commands match Project
test/build/lint commands; done criteria mention evidence + independent
verification; forbidden actions align with Policy denylist; no contradictions
with configured commands; security rules forbid prod secrets/deploys; ambiguous
terms ("make it good") absent unless defined by ACs.
**Acceptance.** A compliant file passes; violations produce specific findings.
**Refs.** §11.
close_reason: Implemented AGENTS.md linter with structured findings and mix conveyor.agents.lint. The linter checks required sections, config command coverage, Done Criteria evidence/independent verification language, Security Rules, Forbidden Actions policy-denylist alignment, command contradictions, and ambiguous phrasing. Verified with Elixir 1.20 + Postgres: focused linter tests passed with 5 tests; full suite passed format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 81 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P0.7.3 AGENTS.md linter tests
description:
**What.** AGENTS.md linter tests.
**Why.** Catch missing commands, vague done criteria, contradictory policy, and
missing security rules.
**Scope.** fixtures (complete / missing-commands / vague-done / contradictory /
no-security) + expected findings.
**Acceptance.** Each fixture yields the expected findings; CI green.
**Refs.** §23 (AGENTS.md linter tests).
close_reason: Added AGENTS.md linter fixtures for complete, missing-command, vague-done, contradictory, and no-security cases with exact expected findings; verified with focused linter tests and full Elixir 1.20 gate.

title: P0.8 — Artifact projector
description:
# P0.8 — Artifact projector

**What.** `Conveyor.Artifacts.Projector` behaviour with a local-disk backend:
content-addressed blob store, idempotent run-directory projection, the canonical
`RunBundle` manifest (`bundle_root_sha256`), and `mix conveyor.report`.

**Why / background.** Postgres is the source of truth; disk is a READ-ONLY
projection for inspectability and PR attachment. Evidence exports are product
artifacts, not debug logs. The behaviour leaves a clean seam for future S3/R2
backends and DuckDB analytics, but Phase 1 ships only local disk. Projection must
verify content-addressed blob digests BEFORE writing the human-friendly tree and
must never treat a projection path as trusted input.

**Scope.** Projector behaviour + local backend; blob store (.conveyor/blobs/
sha256/...) + digest verification; idempotent run-dir projection
(manifest/dossier/evidence/...); RunBundle manifest (excludes non-deterministic
fields from root digest); Artifact resource integration (path = projection,
sha256 = identity, sensitivity, schema_version); `mix conveyor.report`.

**Acceptance.** manifest/dossier/evidence/provenance paths regenerate
idempotently from DB records; every projected artifact has a recorded content
digest; the same run record recreates the same tree + checksums.

**Refs.** §6.4, §9.3, §25 P0.8. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (RunBundle never-cut).

title: P0.8.1 Projector behaviour + local-disk backend
description:
**What.** `Conveyor.Artifacts.Projector` behaviour + local-disk backend.
**Why.** Postgres is truth; disk is a read-only projection. The behaviour leaves a
seam for future S3/R2 backends; Phase 1 ships only local disk for a simple OSS/
headless story.
**Scope.** Projector behaviour; LocalDisk backend writing under .conveyor/;
pluggable backend selection.
**Acceptance.** Behaviour defined; local backend writes the run tree.
**Refs.** §6.4, §8.

title: P0.8.2 Content-addressed blob store + digest verification
description:
**What.** Content-addressed blob store + digest verification.
**Why.** Artifact bytes are stored content-addressably (.conveyor/blobs/sha256/..)
before projection; Artifact.sha256 is identity, path is just a projection.
**Scope.** blob write/read by sha256; digest verification on read; sharded dir
layout.
**Acceptance.** A blob round-trips by digest; a tampered blob fails verification.
**Refs.** §6.4.

title: P0.8.3 Idempotent run-directory projection
description:
**What.** Idempotent run-directory projection.
**Why.** Projection regeneration must be idempotent and must verify blobs FIRST,
never trusting a projection path as input.
**Scope.** write .conveyor/runs/<run_attempt_id>/ (manifest/dossier/evidence/
review/gate/diff/commands/codescent/canary) from DB records + blobs; stable
ordering; exclude non-deterministic fields from digests.
**Acceptance.** Same record → same tree + checksums on regeneration.
**Refs.** §6.4.

title: P0.8.4 RunBundle manifest + bundle_root_sha256
description:
**What.** RunBundle manifest + bundle_root_sha256.
**Why.** The run directory gets a single canonical identity (Conveyor's first
trust anchor) so every PR body, dossier, gate result, and approval references one
digest.
**Scope.** conveyor.run_bundle@1 manifest (entries w/ path/kind/sha256/size/
sensitivity/schema_version); bundle_root_sha256 = sha256(canonical entries),
excluding timestamps/host paths/non-deterministic ordering.
**Acceptance.** Identical runs produce identical bundle_root_sha256; excluded
fields don't affect the digest.
**Refs.** §6.4.

title: P0.8.5 Artifact resource integration (sensitivity + identity)
description:
**What.** Artifact resource integration (kind/sensitivity/schema_version + path vs
identity).
**Why.** Tie projected files to Artifact rows; enforce that sensitive/quarantined
blobs are never projected.
**Scope.** create Artifact rows on projection; sensitivity gating (public/internal/
sensitive/redacted/quarantined); record producer/subject_kind/schema_version.
**Acceptance.** Sensitive blobs are not written to run dirs; every projected file
has an Artifact row + digest.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.4, §16 (redaction).

title: P0.8.6 mix conveyor.report
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.report RUN_ATTEMPT_ID`.
**Why.** Regenerate dossier/manifest/evidence/review/gate/diff/pr_body so the
report is useful even outside LiveView (headless-first).
**Scope.** CLI invoking the projector; print summary; emit machine-readable paths.
**Acceptance.** Regenerates all artifacts for a run attempt idempotently.
**Refs.** §9.3.

title: Future roadmap (deferred)
description:
# Future roadmap (deferred)

Container for the deliberately-deferred roadmap. These stubs exist so the trail
from the Phase-0/1 tracer bullet to the long-term autonomous factory is visible
in the tracker WITHOUT cluttering ready work. Phase 0/1 seeds the data model and
evidence fields these phases need; do NOT build them now.

**Refs.** PHASE-0-1 plan §6.2 (deferred resources), §26 (deferred roadmap hooks).

title: (DEFER) Phase 2 — Decomposition + approval gate
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 2 — Decomposition + approval gate

Spec agent converts a handoff-ready plan into Epics/Slices/Briefs; critic agent
audits contracts; human approves/tweaks the Slice breakdown before execution. The
plan compiler graduates from audit to GENERATION, but audit remains the gate.
Phase 0/1 already builds the audit + traceability seam this depends on.

**Do not build now.** Documented future hook only. **Refs.** §26 Phase 2.

title: (DEFER) Phase 3 — Parallel fleet + merge queue
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 3 — Parallel fleet + merge queue

Dispatcher selects ready Slices; WorkerPool runs isolated containers
concurrently; MergeQueue integrates into dev then a phase gate promotes to main.
Conflict domains + likely_files (captured in Phase 1) feed scheduling. Earns its
place ONLY after the single-Slice loop proves gate honesty + adapter stability.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 3, §27 (swarm-readiness fields).

title: (DEFER) Phase 4 — Verification pyramid
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 4 — Verification pyramid

Slice gate stays fast; Epic gate adds integration/e2e, property + mutation tests,
adversarial red-team review; Phase gate adds full regression, dependency/security
audit, and a human digest. The Phase-1 gate stage model is already designed to
support epic/phase gates.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 4, §17.

title: (DEFER) Phase 5 — Autonomy + self-healing
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 5 — Autonomy + self-healing

Watchdog detects silence/loops/repeated failures/no-progress; retry budgets
escalate to a supervisor agent, re-plan, park, or stop-the-line. Autonomy level
rises ONLY after measured gate reliability (design law 4). Phase 1 records the
RunBudget + failure-taxonomy data this needs.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 5, §12.2 (budget), §20.

title: (DEFER) Phase 6 — Economic governor + observability
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 6 — Economic governor + observability

Cost ledger, budget caps, rate-limit-aware credentials, runaway kill-switch;
LiveView cost meters + critical path + parked queue; object-store artifact backend
(S3/R2) behind the Projector behaviour + cross-run analytics (e.g. DuckDB over the
evidence blobs). Phase 1 ships the Projector seam + bounded metrics for this.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 6, §6.4, §8.1.

title: (DEFER) Phase 7 — Learning loop
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 7 — Learning loop

Structured memory + pgvector recall; prompt-template optimization based on
first-pass success / rework rounds / context-pack misses / review rejection / cost
per success; factory retrospective proposes AGENTS.md, policy, plan-template, and
prompt-template updates. Phase 1 records prompt template versions + retrospective
data this needs.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 7.

title: (DEFER) Phase 8 — Throughput upgrades
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 8 — Throughput upgrades

Interface-stub parallelism; swarm dry-run simulator; agent reputation routing;
conflict heatmap; BEAM distribution for horizontal scale; optional adapters to
Beads, GitHub Issues, Linear, OpenHands, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Aider,
Goose. The AgentRunner behaviour + ExternalTaskRef spec keep these seams open.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 8, §15.

title: (DEFER) Deferred Ash resources (schema specs only)
description:
# (DEFER) Deferred Ash resources (schema specs only)

These resources have documented schema sketches, invariants, and expected event
types under docs/future-schemas/ (see P0.3.14) but NO migrations until a phase
uses them (promotion rule §6.2): WorkspacePool (pooled/warm workspaces), TaskClaim
(multi-agent claim semantics), MergeQueueItem (dev/main integration queue),
BudgetLedger (economic governor beyond Phase-1 RunBudget), AgentReputation
(empirical model/adapter routing), Memory (pgvector institutional recall),
ExternalTaskRef (adapter to Beads/GitHub Issues/Linear).

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §6.2.
